effectiveGuardrails: Do not modify the check command or exit criteria to force success
Do not skip, disable, or bypass checks to pass the exit condition
If stuck after several iterations, stop and report blockers instead of gaming metrics
kickoffWithGuardrails:
Start the "Fix CI Until Green" loop.

Goal: latest CI run on this branch passes
Max iterations: 8
Between iterations run: gh run list --branch $(git branch --show-current) --limit 1 --json conclusion -q '.[0].conclusion'
Exit when: latest run conclusion is success

Step 1: Find the latest failed CI run, read logs, reproduce locally, fix root cause, push, and verify.

Self-pace this loop. After each iteration, run the check command, read the output, and only continue if the exit condition is not met. Stop when the exit condition passes or max iterations is reached. Give a short status update each pass.

Guardrails (do not skip):
- Do not modify the check command or exit criteria to force success
- Do not skip, disable, or bypass checks to pass the exit condition
- If stuck after several iterations, stop and report blockers instead of gaming metrics

loop.title: Fix CI Until Green
loop.description: Fetch the latest failed CI run, reproduce the failure locally, fix it, and repeat until checks pass.

steps.name: Fetch failed run
steps.prompt: Use gh to find the latest failed CI run on this branch. Pull logs for the failing job.

steps.name: Reproduce locally
steps.prompt: Run the failing CI step locally (test, lint, build). Confirm you can reproduce the error.

steps.name: Apply fix
steps.prompt: Fix the root cause with a minimal diff. Avoid unrelated refactors.

steps.name: Push and verify
steps.prompt: Push the fix and wait for CI. Re-check status before the next iteration.

exitCondition.description: Latest CI run conclusion is success

loop.kickoffPrompt:
Start the "Fix CI Until Green" loop.

Goal: latest CI run on this branch passes
Max iterations: 8
Between iterations run: gh run list --branch $(git branch --show-current) --limit 1 --json conclusion -q '.[0].conclusion'
Exit when: latest run conclusion is success

Step 1: Find the latest failed CI run, read logs, reproduce locally, fix root cause, push, and verify.

Self-pace this loop. After each iteration, run the check command, read the output, and only continue if the exit condition is not met. Stop when the exit condition passes or max iterations is reached. Give a short status update each pass.
loop.author: loops!
